title: Operating Systems Labs 2-4 (x86 kernel)
summary: A four-lab OS course project where each lab is a self-contained bootable x86 kernel. Lab 1 (bootloader) is pre-implemented; Labs 2-4 add system calls, process management, and process synchronization respectively. Each lab shares the same three-subsystem structure: VGA framebuffer routines, kernel IRQ/syscall handlers, and a user-space I/O library.
instructionPreview: This is a four-lab undergraduate operating-systems course project. Each lab builds a bootable x86 OS image from C and assembly source. The labs progress from bootloader through system calls, process management, and process synchronization. Lab 1 is already complete; you must implement Labs 2–4.
instruction:
This is a four-lab undergraduate operating-systems course project. Each lab builds a bootable x86 OS image from C and assembly source. The labs progress from bootloader through system calls, process management, and process synchronization. Lab 1 is already complete; you must implement Labs 2–4.

Your working directory is `/workspace`. The project code is already present with stub implementations (`/* TODO: implement */`) across multiple subsystems. You must implement all stubbed-out modules so that all tests pass.

## Modules to Implement

### Lab 2 — System Calls (`lab2-231220140/`)

- **Kernel IRQ Handlers** (`kernel/kernel/irqHandle.c`): Implement the keyboard input handler that processes scan codes, handles special keys (backspace, enter), and stores characters in the key buffer. Implement the system call handlers for printing characters to VGA, reading characters from the key buffer, reading strings, and reading the current time from the CMOS/RTC hardware registers. (~5 functions)

- **VGA Display** (`kernel/kernel/vga.c`): Implement screen-clearing and screen-scrolling routines that operate on the VGA framebuffer at address 0xb8000. (~2 functions)

- **User-Space I/O Library** (`lib/syscall.c`): Implement the printf function with support for `%d`, `%x`, `%s`, and `%c` format specifiers. Implement the supporting conversion functions: decimal-to-string, hexadecimal-to-string, and string-copy-to-buffer. Implement the sleep wrapper that invokes the sleep system call. (~5 functions)

### Lab 3 — Process Management (`lab3-231220140/`)

- **Process Scheduler** (`kernel/kernel/irqHandle.c`): Implement round-robin process scheduling, context switching between processes (saving/restoring CPU state via the TSS and process stacks), and timer-driven preemptive scheduling that decrements sleep counters. Implement system call handlers for fork (duplicating a process), exec (loading and running a new program), sleep (blocking for a duration), exit (terminating a process), and printing to VGA with proper segment selector handling. (~8 functions)

- **VGA Display** (`kernel/kernel/vga.c`): Implement screen-clearing and screen-scrolling routines for the VGA framebuffer. (~2 functions)

- **User-Space I/O Library** (`lib/syscall.c`): Implement printf with format specifier support and the decimal/hex/string conversion helpers. (~4 functions)

### Lab 4 — Process Synchronization (`lab4-231220140/`)

- **Process Scheduler & IRQ Handlers** (`kernel/kernel/irqHandle.c`): Implement process scheduling, context switching, timer handling, and keyboard input handling with device-level blocking. Implement stdout/stdin system call handlers that interact with device state. Implement process lifecycle syscalls (fork, exec, sleep, exit). Implement semaphore operations: initialization, wait (P operation with blocking), post (V operation with wakeup), and destroy. Implement shared variable operations: create, destroy, read, and write. (~16 functions)

- **VGA Display** (`kernel/kernel/vga.c`): Implement screen-clearing and screen-scrolling for the VGA framebuffer. (~2 functions)

- **User-Space I/O Library** (`lib/syscall.c`): Implement printf with full format specifier support. Implement scanf with format parsing for `%d`, `%x`, `%s`, and `%c`, including whitespace matching, decimal string-to-integer parsing, hexadecimal string-to-integer parsing, and width-limited string parsing. Implement user-space shared variable wrappers (create, destroy, read, write). (~13 functions)

## Test-Coupled Patterns

The test suite reads source files and checks for the presence of specific function names and patterns. The following must appear in the corresponding `irqHandle.c` files:

- Lab 2: `sysPrint` with VGA framebuffer reference `0xb8000`, `sysGetChar` with `keyBuffer` reference, `sysnow` with CMOS port `0x70`
- Lab 3: `sysFork`, `sysExec`, `sysSleep`, `sysExit`, `schedule`
- Lab 4: `sysSemInit`, `sysSemWait`, `sysSemPost`, `sysSVarCreate` or `sysSharedVar`

moduleDag.description: A four-lab OS course project where each lab is a self-contained bootable x86 kernel. Lab 1 (bootloader) is pre-implemented; Labs 2-4 add system calls, process management, and process synchronization respectively. Each lab shares the same three-subsystem structure: VGA framebuffer routines, kernel IRQ/syscall handlers, and a user-space I/O library.

nodes.label: Lab2 VGA Display
nodes.description: Implements clearScreen (blanks the 80x25 VGA framebuffer at 0xb8000) and scrollScreen (shifts all rows up by one and clears the bottom row). These primitives are called by every kernel print path and must exist before any character output works.

nodes.label: Lab2 User-Space I/O Library
nodes.description: Implements user-space printf with %d/%x/%s/%c support and the helper conversion routines dec2Str, hex2Str, str2Str, plus the blocking sleep wrapper that uses the SYS_SETTIMEFLAG/SYS_GETTIMEFLAG syscalls.

nodes.label: Lab2 Kernel IRQ and Syscall Handlers
nodes.description: Implements KeyboardHandle (scan-code decoding, backspace/enter handling, key buffer), sysPrint (VGA character output), sysGetChar and sysGetStr (blocking keyboard reads), and sysnow (CMOS/RTC time via port 0x70). Calls scrollScreen from vga.c on line wrap.

nodes.label: Lab3 VGA Display
nodes.description: Re-implementation of clearScreen and scrollScreen for the lab3 kernel binary. Functionally identical to lab2_vga; must be present before lab3's irqHandle can call scrollScreen during sysPrint or process-switch output.

nodes.label: Lab3 User-Space I/O Library
nodes.description: Re-implementation of printf and the dec2Str/hex2Str/str2Str helpers for lab3. Adapted from lab2's version; requires working lab2 syscall infrastructure as the conceptual model, and must be combined with lab3's kernel before tests can pass.

nodes.label: Lab3 Process Scheduler and Syscall Handlers
nodes.description: Implements round-robin schedule(), context-switching contextSwitch(), and timer-driven preemptive timerHandle(). Adds process lifecycle syscalls: sysFork (PCB duplication with stack fixup), sysExec (load and run new image), sysSleep (block with sleep counter), sysExit (terminate process), and sysPrint (with correct segment selector handling).

nodes.label: Lab4 VGA Display
nodes.description: Re-implementation of clearScreen and scrollScreen for the lab4 kernel binary. Must be in place before lab4's irqHandle can produce any VGA output during syscall handling or device-level I/O operations.

nodes.label: Lab4 User-Space I/O Library (with scanf and shared vars)
nodes.description: Extends lab3's printf with a full scanf implementation supporting %d/%x/%s/%c, whitespace matching, and width-limited string parsing. Adds user-space wrappers for shared-variable operations (create, destroy, read, write).

nodes.label: Lab4 Semaphores, Shared Vars, and Full IRQ Handlers
nodes.description: Extends lab3's scheduler and process syscalls with device-level blocking for stdin/stdout. Adds semaphore operations: sysSemInit, sysSemWait (P with blocking), sysSemPost (V with wakeup), sysSemDestroy. Adds shared-variable kernel operations: sysSVarCreate, sysSVarDestroy, sysSVarRead, sysSVarWrite.

edges.label: irqHandle.c calls scrollScreen() and clearScreen() from vga.c for every print path
all lab3 work begins after lab2 kernel syscalls are complete; lab3_vga is the first lab3 step
lab3 user-space lib is adapted directly from lab2's printf/conversion implementation
lab3 user-space requires working lab2 syscall infrastructure as the conceptual model before meaningful implementation
lab3 scheduler and process syscalls extend lab2's syscall handler framework (same irqHandle structure)
lab3 irqHandle.c calls scrollScreen() during sysPrint output; vga.c must exist first
all lab4 work begins after lab3 process management kernel is complete
lab4 user-space lib extends lab3's printf with scanf and shared-variable wrappers
lab4 user-space requires lab3 kernel syscall semantics as the conceptual foundation
lab4 semaphore and shared-variable handlers extend lab3's scheduler and process lifecycle syscalls
lab4 irqHandle.c calls scrollScreen() for device-level stdout output; vga.c must exist first
